title: For how long did the writer give up smoking?

meanings.usage: 指人的性情或情绪状态
描述带有负罪感的动作
指强烈地劝某人做某事
指把东西藏起来不让别人看到
指觉得有趣或好笑的状态
描述符合常规或标准的状态
指让人放松或平息情绪

examples.en: He decided to give up smoking last year.

phrases.usage: 镇定我的神经，使我放松

examples.en: She took a deep breath to calm her nerves.
examples.zh: 她深呼吸让自己放松下来。

phrases.usage: 继续做某事，坚持做某事

examples.en: They kept on offering me cigarettes.

phrases.usage: 超出我的承受能力，我无法忍受

examples.en: The pain was more than I could bear.
examples.zh: 疼痛超出了我的承受能力。

phrases.phrase: make no effort to do something
phrases.usage: 没有努力做某事，毫不费力做某事

examples.en: They made no effort to hide their amusement.
examples.zh: 他们毫不掩饰自己的笑意。

examples.en: He lit a cigarette to calm his nerves.
examples.zh: 他点了一根烟来镇定神经。

phrases.usage: 恢复正常，回到正常状态

examples.en: Things had returned to normal once more.
As Brian pointed out, it is the easiest thing.
examples.zh: 正如布莱恩指出的，这是最简单的事。

examples.en: I produced a packet of sweets from my pocket.
examples.zh: 我从口袋里掏出一包糖果。

phrases.phrase: the easiest thing in the world

examples.en: It is the easiest thing in the world to give up smoking.
examples.zh: 戒烟是世界上最容易的事。

grammar.title: 一般过去时与过去进行时
grammar.definition: 一般过去时描述在过去某个时间完成的动作；过去进行时描述在过去某个时间正在进行的动作。两者可以结合使用。
grammar.structure: When引导的时间状语从句中，从句用一般过去时，主句用过去进行时；或者主句用一般过去时，从句用过去进行时。
grammar.usage: 用于描述过去同时发生的动作，或一个动作发生时另一个动作正在进行。

examples.en: When my old friend Brian urged me to accept a cigarette, I took one.
examples.zh: 当我的老朋友布莱恩劝我接受一支烟时，我接了一支。

examples.en: While I was smoking, my wife was watching me.
examples.zh: 当我抽烟时，我的妻子正在看着我。

examples.en: After reading an article, I lit a cigarette.
examples.zh: 读完一篇文章后，我点了一根烟。

grammar.title: For + 时间段 + 过去时
grammar.definition: 用for引出时间段，表示某个动作或状态持续了多长时间，常与过去时连用。
grammar.structure: for + 一段时间 + 一般过去时
grammar.usage: 描述过去某事持续的时间长度，常与表示戒烟、等待等持续性动作连用。

examples.en: For a whole week I did not smoke at all.
examples.zh: 整整一周我一根烟都没抽。

examples.en: I stayed there for three hours.
examples.zh: 我在那里待了三个小时。

examples.en: She worked in London for five years.

grammar.title: too...to 和 more than + 情感动词
grammar.definition: too + 形容词/副词 + to do something 表示太...以至于不能...；more than + 情感动词表示超过某种情感所能承受的程度。
grammar.structure: too + adj/adv + (for someone) + to do something / more than + 情感动词(could/bear等)
grammar.usage: 用于强调某种程度超出承受范围，常用于表达无法忍受的情感。

examples.en: It was more than I could bear.
examples.zh: 这超出了我的承受能力。

examples.en: The box is too heavy to carry.
examples.zh: 这个箱子太重了，搬不动。

examples.en: She was too nervous to speak.
examples.zh: 她太紧张了，说不出话来。

sentencePatterns.pattern: For + 一段时间 + did not + 动词原形

original.en: For a whole week I did not smoke at all.
original.zh: 整整一周我一根烟都没抽。

imitations.en: For a month I did not drink coffee.
imitations.zh: 整整一个月我没喝咖啡。

imitations.en: For two hours he did not say a word.
imitations.zh: 整整两个小时他一句话都没说。

sentencePatterns.pattern: After + doing something, 主语 + 动词

original.en: After reading an article, I lit a cigarette.
original.zh: 读完一篇文章后，我点了一根烟。

imitations.en: After finishing my homework, I watched TV.
imitations.zh: 做完作业后，我看了电视。

imitations.en: After getting up, she made breakfast.

sentencePatterns.pattern: It is the easiest thing in the world to do something

original.en: It is the easiest thing in the world to give up smoking.
original.zh: 戒烟是世界上最容易的事。

imitations.en: It is the easiest thing in the world to learn English.
imitations.zh: 学英语是世界上最容易的事。

imitations.en: It is the hardest thing in the world to wake up early.
imitations.zh: 早起是世界上最难的事。

sentencePatterns.pattern: When + 一般过去时, 主语 + 过去时

original.en: When my old friend Brian urged me to accept a cigarette, it was more than I could bear.
original.zh: 当我的老朋友布莱恩劝我接受一支烟时，我无法忍受了。

imitations.en: When he offered me a drink, it was more than I could bear.
imitations.zh: 当他给我递饮料时，我忍不住了。

imitations.en: When she asked me to dance, I felt nervous.
imitations.zh: 当她邀请我跳舞时，我很紧张。
